title: alnus glutinosa f. maculata
intro: Alnus glutinosa f. maculata, also known as spotted alder, is a variant of the common alder tree (Alnus glutinosa) characterized by its distinctive spotted leaves. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Alnus glutinosa f. maculata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to soothe respiratory issues and calm digestive problems.
